label: Front
description: Connect to Front to read and manage shared inboxes, conversations, and customer messages.

form.title: Connect Front
form.subtitle: Paste your Front API token to give Anton access to your shared inboxes and conversations.

methods.label: API Token
methods.description: Paste a Front API token generated from Settings → Developers → API tokens. The simplest and fastest way to connect — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Front API Token

Front API tokens are personal tokens tied to your account. They are shown once at creation time, so copy yours before closing the dialog.

### Steps

1. Log in to Front and click your **workspace name** in the top-left corner.
2. Go to **Settings** (gear icon) → **Developers** → **API tokens**.
3. Click **New token**.
4. Give the token a name like `Anton` so you can identify it later.
5. Select the **scopes** Anton needs — at minimum `shared_resources:read` for reading conversations, or broader scopes if you want write access.
6. Click **Create token**. Copy the token string — it is only shown once.
7. Paste it into the **API Token** field below.

### Notes

- Tokens are scoped to the user who created them; they can only access inboxes and conversations that user can see.
- If you need org-wide access, ask a Front admin to create the token or use an admin account.
- To revoke, return to **Settings → Developers → API tokens** and delete the entry.
- Front's API base URL is `https://api2.frontapp.com`; you can leave the field below at its default unless your account uses a custom endpoint.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Found in Front → Settings → Developers → API tokens. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: API Base URL
fields.description: Leave as default unless your Front account uses a custom or regional API endpoint.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own App)
methods.description: Use a Front OAuth app for delegated access. Requires creating an OAuth application in Front's developer settings and supplying a client ID and secret.
methods.how_to:
## Connect Front via OAuth 2.0

OAuth is suitable when you want token refresh handled automatically or you're building a multi-user integration. You must first create an OAuth application in Front.

### Steps

1. Log in to Front and go to **Settings → Developers → OAuth applications**.
2. Click **Create OAuth application**.
3. Enter a name like `Anton` and set the redirect URI to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect).
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** from the application detail page.
5. Paste both values into the fields below.
6. Click **Submit** — a browser window will open Front's consent screen.
7. Approve the requested scopes. Front redirects back to Anton with an authorization code, which Anton exchanges for access and refresh tokens.

### Notes

- Refresh tokens allow Anton to reconnect without re-prompting you after the access token expires.
- Revoke access in Front under **Settings → Developers → OAuth applications** by removing the app or revoking the token.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Front → Settings → Developers → OAuth applications → your app's Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; shown once in the OAuth application detail page.
